result:
**T2 complete.** All three deliverables are in the working directory, plus `estimates.csv` as a checkable artifact.

**What was found.** Using projoint's estimator (profile-level, corrected AMCEs, analytical SEs auto-clustered on respondent `id` — confirmed via `fit$cluster_by == "id"`), the largest effects on choosing a residential profile are: high crime −25.1 pp, a 75-minute daily drive −23.7 pp, and housing at 40% of income −19.8 pp; good schools (+11.6 pp) and small towns / mixed-use suburbs (+14–16 pp) attract, while partisanship and racial composition are null. All 400 respondents × 8 tasks used.

**How the orchestration ran.**
- **Plan shown first**, then **one delegation to fast-worker (Sonnet)** — mechanical, fully-specified work per the routing rule; no Opus/Codex needed. It wrote and ran `script.R`, and returned the estimates table plus two honest deviations worth noting: projoint 1.1.1's default is **analytical** cluster-robust SEs (CR2, with projoint's own automatic fallback to Stata/HC1 for 3 attributes — a package behavior, not an error), not bootstrap, so the pipeline is deterministic (`set.seed(20260711)` is still set defensively); and it reports the **corrected** AMCE, projoint's headline estimand.
- **One revision cycle, applied by me directly**: the first figure's panel was squeezed to nothing by long level labels, and a second pass had floating-point scientific notation on the x-axis. I wrapped labels/strips, widened to 9×9 in, switched to `geom_errorbar(orientation="y")`, and fixed the axis breaks. Verified the final PNG visually and by dimensions (2700×2700 @ 300 dpi).
- I wrote `report.md` myself (~200 words, caption under the figure reference), retaining integration ownership. Total: 1 of 3 allowed delegations; advisor tool was unavailable, so I proceeded on the low-risk mechanical path.
